chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Const. Petition No. D-5110 of 2025 Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) For orders as to maintainability of petition. 14th April, 2026. Mr. Ashfaq Rafiq Janjua, Advocate for petitioner M/S Hakim Ali Shaikh and Sagheer Ahmed Abbasi, A.A.G.Sindh >>>>> <<<<< At the very outset, learned AAG Sindh submitted that he has not received copy of instant petition along with its annexures. Learned counsel for the petitioner, while undertaking to provide such copies, submitted that at the time of filing the petition, he had already delivered the same to the office of the Advocate General, Sindh. Perusal of the file, however, does not reveal any endorsement to affirm the submission made by learned counsel for the petitioner. It is, nevertheless, a common practice that when a petition is filed before the Court, sets of copies are provided to the concerned offices. We have noted in a number of cases that this practice has been deprecated either by the office or by the learned Advocates themselves. In the circumstance, office is directed not to entertain fresh cases in future unless copies are delivered in advance to the office of Advocate General Sindh, Prosecutor General Sindh and Additional Attorney General for Pakistan duly acknowledged by its/ their authorized representative/official. Let a copy of this order be communicated to Additional Registrars of all allied Benches in the Province of Sindh for strict compliance.Learned MIT-II to ensure compliance. To come up on 10.06.2026. Judge Head of Const. Benches Aamir/PS Judge
